timeframe.expansion_reason: Strict 72h window (May 16-19) yielded 2 uncover items not previously published (Musk verdict May 18, Google I/O keynote May 19). Most May 13-18 items covered in prior bulletins. No further expansion needed — 4 items found across window.

published_items.title: Google I/O 2026 opens: Gemini Intelligence for Android, Googlebooks, and Android XR glasses confirmed
key_facts: Gemini Intelligence agentic Android layer: background task automation, Magic Pointer, custom widgets, Rambler voice cleanup
Gemini Intelligence rolling out summer 2026 on Samsung Galaxy and Pixel devices
Auto-browse in Chrome for Android requires AI Pro/Ultra subscription; limited US rollout end of June 2026
Googlebooks: Android/Aluminium OS laptops from Acer, ASUS, Dell, HP, Lenovo — fall 2026
Android XR glasses: Samsung, Warby Parker, Gentle Monster, XREAL partnerships confirmed — late 2026
Main I/O keynote (May 19 10AM PT) new Gemini model details not yet indexed at run time
caveats: blog.google primary returned 404 from run environment; confirmed via Engadget and NextWeb T3 summaries
Main keynote model announcement details pending indexing; item covers Android Show pre-announcements only
Gemini Intelligence availability is summer 2026 — not yet shipped
Auto-browse: US only, limited rollout, requires paid subscription tier

published_items.title: Federal jury dismisses Musk's $134B suit against OpenAI on statute of limitations
key_facts: Jury deliberated less than two hours; dismissed all claims on statute of limitations
Jury found Musk knew of OpenAI for-profit shift as early as 2021; suit filed 2024 exceeded 3-year limit
$134B in claimed damages; breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment claims both dismissed
Microsoft aiding-and-abetting count also dismissed
Merits of underlying charitable trust claim never decided
Musk announces appeal to 9th Circuit; judge indicated readiness to dismiss appeal
Musk called judge 'terrible activist' and verdict a 'calendar technicality' on X
caveats: Verdict on statute of limitations — does not resolve whether charitable trust obligation existed
Appeal pending; outcome may shift the picture

published_items.title: OpenHuman open-source personal agent reaches 17.3k GitHub stars in six days
key_facts: Released v0.53.43 on 2026-05-13; 17.3k GitHub stars at run time (2026-05-19)
118+ third-party OAuth integrations: Gmail, GitHub, Slack, Notion, Stripe, Google Calendar, Google Drive, Linear, Jira
20-minute auto-polling: agent fetches new data from all connected accounts autonomously
Local Memory Tree stored in SQLite and Markdown; Obsidian-compatible vault
TokenJuice layer: vendor claims up to 80% token reduction (no independent benchmark)
Data stays on-device and encrypted; no vendor cloud storage
Subconscious loop independently decides actions based on user priorities
caveats: 80% token reduction claim is vendor-claimed from README; no independent benchmark
Simultaneous OAuth access to email, code repos, calendars, and payments creates broad attack surface
Stars are not adoption — no telemetry on production usage
v0.53.43 version number suggests significant prior internal development; project history not fully disclosed

cross_references.role: corroborating and contradicting — confirms features; flags privacy surface critically

published_items.title: Claude Code v2.1.144 adds /resume for background sessions and per-session model switching
key_facts: Released 2026-05-19 at 00:48 UTC
/resume support for background sessions (started via claude --bg or agent view)
Background sessions appear in agent view with 'bg' indicator
Elapsed duration shown in subagent completion notifications
/model now scopes change to current session only; press 'd' in picker to set new-session default
Plugin browse/discover panes show when plugin was last updated
Terminology: 'extra usage' renamed to 'usage credits'; /extra-usage → /usage-credits (old alias still works)
